description: Conditional prompt-to-Python-capability retrieval, routing, and verification experiment space.

implemented_components: existing exact word character FTS and hybrid retrieval profiles
deterministic compatibility and verified seed execution
design_only_until_an_adapter_and_receipt: most MinHash SimHash ANN service reranker and graph arms
full configuration to end-to-end benchmark orchestration
runner_coverage.warning: A valid scheduled configuration is not necessarily connected to an executable adapter.

dimensions.description: Smallest independently retrievable catalog entity.
Minimum evidence level allowed into candidate supply.
Lineage rule for supervised, calibrated, optimized, or historical state; the holdout is never a fitting source.
Observed or derived text planes presented to retrieval channels.
How long evidence is split and re-associated with its entity.
Deterministic normalization applied before lexical features and hashing.
Versioned deterministic labels, tags, keys, and structural features.
Non-generative NLP lane for keyphrases, topics, intent, tone, and domain facets.
Source of additional purpose, use-case, problem, solution, and limitation descriptions.
Method used to turn a user prompt into searchable action, object, artifact, constraint, domain, and reuse facets.
Additional prompt variants used as independently attributable retrieval queries.
Deterministic keys used to reduce the candidate universe before ranking.
How multiple exact and approximate blocking lanes form candidate supply.
Approximate hashing family; each lane is evaluated for recall loss and candidate reduction.
Shared coarse/medium/fine setting translated into family-specific bands, permutations, bits, or thresholds.
Sparse or exact candidate ranking lane.
Dense embedding model; immutable Hub revision is resolved and recorded at run time.
Fields encoded together or independently by the dense model.
Query/document prefix or instruction policy, always stored with the vector digest.
Pooling policy; model-default is the reference and alternatives are explicit ablations.
Native or model-supported Matryoshka dimension followed by re-normalization.
Embedding inference/output precision; finite and nonzero vectors are mandatory.
Vector comparison metric; normalization state is recorded independently.
Exact or approximate dense-vector storage/search implementation.
Portable low/medium/high search effort translated into index-specific parameters.
Method used to combine independently attributable exact, sparse, dense, LSH, and graph ranks.
Versioned typed edges eligible for expansion; unknown compatibility never becomes a positive edge.
Graph search applied after initial candidate retrieval.
Optional shortlist reranker, evaluated at frozen candidate depths.
Number of candidates scored by the optional reranker.
Placement and strictness of version, platform, policy, resource, and named-port checks.
Calibrated decision between reuse, no reuse, prohibited, unknown, and insufficient evidence.
Optional model used only to propose reviewable derived descriptions, labels, edges, or examples.
Model allowed to select only retrieved operation and adapter IDs inside a constrained route structure.
Runtime is an independent factor so backend differences are not credited to the model or retriever.
Weight/runtime format; actual artifact bytes and peak VRAM are measured rather than inferred.
Thinking is varied explicitly and never allowed to silently change token budgets.
Bounded candidate context used for planning; advertised maximum model context is not assumed feasible.
Number of allowlisted candidates presented to the constrained planner.
How a model proposal is constrained before deterministic validation.
Strongest required verification gate for an accepted result.

constraints.reason: without a dense lane, embedding/index factors are neutral and a lexical lane supplies candidates
dense vectors must be queried through a declared exact or approximate index
the T4/P100 qualification lane uses EmbeddingGemma FP32 and rejects non-finite vectors
dimension truncation is valid only for a model that explicitly supports this Matryoshka size
dimension truncation is valid only for a model that explicitly supports this Matryoshka size
dimension truncation is valid only for a model that explicitly supports this Matryoshka size
64 dimensions are kept only for explicitly registered Matryoshka models
Hamming comparison is defined here only for binary codes in the Faiss LSH arm
binary vector output is evaluated with Hamming distance
graph expansion cannot run without a declared edge supply
there is no graph to expand
model-backed features and planning require a measured runtime
a dormant model server is not part of a deterministic arm
GGUF is evaluated through a GGUF runtime, not the Transformers reference
the initial Unsloth qualification lane is limited to FP16 and NF4
llama.cpp server trials use an exact recorded GGUF artifact
Ollama trials use an exact recorded local model artifact
Gemma 4 E4B is qualified on a 16 GB Kaggle GPU only through a quantized arm
LLM-derived descriptions remain candidates and name the model that generated them
an enrichment model is not loaded when no description or query feature uses it
model-derived query facets name their generator and runtime
model-derived query expansions name their generator and runtime
an LLM reranker must name and meter its model
grammar, function-call, and free-form output controls are model-only factors
LSH resolution is neutral when no LSH family is selected
rerank depth is neutral when no reranker is selected
blocking logic has no candidate supply when exact and approximate blockers are absent
the Faiss IndexLSH arm compares binary codes with Hamming distance
ANN effort is neutral for exhaustive or absent vector indexes
task-fitted, calibrated, optimized, and historical state must name an allowed development lineage
successful prior queries must precede the evaluated request
accepted-route edges must precede the evaluated request
supervised labels must never be fitted on the holdout
query intent fitting must never use the holdout
learned blocking must never use the holdout
prompt optimization must never use the holdout
fusion fitting must never use the holdout
abstention fitting and calibration must never use the holdout
